nodes.x: Deliver me, O Lord, from the evil man: preserve me from the violent man;
Keep me, O Lord, from the hands of the wicked; preserve me from the violent man; who have purposed to overthrow my goings.
Deliver me, O my God, out of the hand of the wicked, out of the hand of the unrighteous and cruel man.
Deliver me, O Lord, from mine enemies: I flee unto thee to hide me.
To deliver thee from the way of the evil man, from the man that speaketh froward things;
He delivereth me from mine enemies: yea, thou liftest me up above those that rise up against me: thou hast delivered me from the violent man.
Which imagine mischiefs in their heart; continually are they gathered together for war.
For their heart studieth destruction, and their lips talk of mischief.

Come on, let us deal wisely with them; lest they multiply, and it come to pass, that, when there falleth out any war, they join also unto our enemies, and fight against us, and so get them up out of the land.

We that have this warfare to meet, should keep ourselves prepared for any and every attack of the evil one. It becomes us to draw ourselves together in the bonds of unity, to cling to each other, our covenants and our God. We are called upon not only
They shall run like mighty men; they shall climb the wall like men of war; and they shall march every one on his ways, and they shall not break their ranks:
In the grasp of conflict they blindly cru 3 h Their valour, their strength, and might:
They have sharpened their tongues like a serpent; adders’ poison is under their lips. Selah.
Their poison is like the poison of a serpent: they are like the deaf adder that stoppeth her ear;
At the last it biteth like a serpent, and stingeth like an adder.
Their throat is an open sepulchre; with their tongues they have used deceit; the poison of asps is under their lips:
But the tongue can no man tame; it is an unruly evil, full of deadly poison.
They shall take up serpents; and if they drink any deadly thing, it shall not hurt them; they shall lay hands on the sick, and they shall recover.
Keep me, O Lord, from the hands of the wicked; preserve me from the violent man; who have purposed to overthrow my goings.
Deliver me, O Lord, from the evil man: preserve me from the violent man;
For I have kept the ways of the Lord, and have not wickedly departed from my God.
Preserve me, O God: for in thee do I put my trust.
The Lord shall preserve thee from all evil: he shall preserve thy soul.
Depart from me, ye evildoers: for I will keep the commandments of my God.
The proud have hid a snare for me, and cords; they have spread a net by the wayside; they have set gins for me. Selah.
Keep me from the snares which they have laid for me, and the gins of the workers of iniquity.
The proud have digged pits for me, which are not after thy law.
For he is cast into a net by his own feet, and he walketh upon a snare.
The snare is laid for him in the ground, and a trap for him in the way.
I drew them with cords of a man, with bands of love: and I was to them as they that take off the yoke on their jaws, and I laid meat unto them.
I said unto the Lord, Thou art my God: hear the voice of my supplications, O Lord.
Blessed be the Lord, because he hath heard the voice of my supplications.
Hear the voice of my supplications, when I cry unto thee, when I lift up my hands toward thy holy oracle.
I cried unto the Lord with my voice; with my voice unto the Lord did I make my supplication.
I cried to thee, O Lord; and unto the Lord I made supplication.
The Lord hath heard my supplication; the Lord will receive my prayer.
O God the Lord, the strength of my salvation, thou hast covered my head in the day of battle.
But thou, O Lord, art a shield for me; my glory, and the lifter up of mine head.
For thou hast girded me with strength to battle: them that rose up against me hast thou subdued under me.
For thou hast girded me with strength unto the battle: thou hast subdued under me those that rose up against me.
And now shall mine head be lifted up above mine enemies round about me: therefore will I offer in his tabernacle sacrifices of joy; I will sing, yea, I will sing praises unto the Lord.
Grant not, O Lord, the desires of the wicked: further not his wicked device; lest they exalt themselves. Selah.

I am pleased to have the opportunity of again meeting with the people of Grantsville. In regard to the remarks which we have just heard pertaining to the desires and intentions of the wicked they are true and correct; but at the same time I do not feel any trembling in my knees, do you? It has been said, the wicked rage, and the people imagine a vain thing; and the Lord will have them in…
And then shall that Wicked be revealed, whom the Lord shall consume with the spirit of his mouth, and shall destroy with the brightness of his coming:
The wicked shall see it, and be grieved; he shall gnash with his teeth, and melt away: the desire of the wicked shall perish.
The fear of the wicked, it shall come upon him: but the desire of the righteous shall be granted.
As for the head of those that compass me about, let the mischief of their own lips cover them.
They compassed me about; yea, they compassed me about: but in the name of the Lord I will destroy them.
They compassed me about like bees; they are quenched as the fire of thorns: for in the name of the Lord I will destroy them.
They compassed me about also with words of hatred; and fought against me without a cause.
For innumerable evils have compassed me about: mine iniquities have taken hold upon me, so that I am not able to look up; they are more than the hairs of mine head: therefore my heart faileth me.
Let burning coals fall upon them: let them be cast into the fire; into deep pits, that they rise not up again.
Can one go upon hot coals, and his feet not be burned?
As soon then as they were come to land, they saw a fire of coals there, and fish laid thereon, and bread.
Behold, they shall be as stubble; the fire shall burn them; they shall not deliver themselves from the power of the flame: there shall not be a coal to warm at, nor fire to sit before it.
Before him went the pestilence, and burning coals went forth at his feet.

away a winter's stock in our cellars, and turn the key upon it, and this will actually make some men practically honest, whereas, if your woodpile is out of doors, they may continue to be dishonest. I speak now of the practice of dishonest people, not of their intent. If we can get men to practice honesty, virtue, and holiness, I am fully of the faith that they will become righteous in their…
Let not an evil speaker be established in the earth: evil shall hunt the violent man to overthrow him.
Hear, O earth: behold, I will bring evil upon this people, even the fruit of their thoughts, because they have not hearkened unto my words, nor to my law, but rejected it.
Let him eschew evil, and do good; let him seek peace, and ensue it.
He that deviseth to do evil shall be called a mischievous person.
Mine enemies speak evil of me, When shall he die, and his name perish?
I know that the Lord will maintain the cause of the afflicted, and the right of the poor.
I will also leave in the midst of thee an afflicted and poor people, and they shall trust in the name of the Lord.
Yet setteth he the poor on high from affliction, and maketh him families like a flock.
Blessed is he that considereth the poor: the Lord will deliver him in time of trouble.

And with righteousness shall the Lord God judge the poor, and reprove with equity for the meek of the earth. And he shall smite the earth with the rod of his mouth; and with the breath of his lips shall he slay the wicked.
Surely the righteous shall give thanks unto thy name: the upright shall dwell in thy presence.
Therefore will I give thanks unto thee, O Lord, among the heathen, and sing praises unto thy name.
Therefore I will give thanks unto thee, O Lord, among the heathen, and I will sing praises unto thy name.
Rejoice in the Lord, O ye righteous: for praise is comely for the upright.
Blessed be the name of our God; let us sing to his praise, yea, let us give thanks to his holy name, for he doth work righteousness forever.
I will declare thy name unto my brethren: in the midst of the congregation will I praise thee.
